page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 24 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated high-frequency CPU cores and optimized network throughput deliver a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring high single-core performance without the overhead of local storage.

Benchmark results place the m5zn.2xlarge in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1565.0 and a multi-core score of 5869.0. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is strong at 31.88 GB/sec, while Redis SET operations average 9.28 million operations per second. However, LLM inference performance is limited, with the medium 7B model achieving only 7.44 tokens per second during text generation. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, high-frequency single-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 8 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz, alongside 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, utilizing a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage connectivity. Benchmarks indicate average performance across most tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1565.0 and multi-core score of 5869.0, though it demonstrates strong single-threaded gzip compression at 31.88 GB/sec. Conversely, LLM inference on larger models is weak, with a 7B model yielding 7.44 tokens per second. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and applications requiring high-frequency CPU performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.

tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and optimized network bandwidth for balanced compute workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz
8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 31.88 GB/sec
No local storage or GPU resources included
Suitable for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose applications
